human:
POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:136] > that part of the changeset isn't necessary to accomplish the stated goal of this issue.

It is unrelated.  Its a bug I noticed while working this issue.  The current code will not set an error return code for the process.
v1:
SUMMARY:
This PR removes informational print statements and improves error handling in ScanUtil by switching from stdout to stderr with System.exit(-1). However, the PR title ("stopped printing meta info") is misleading since the changes actively REMOVE meta info output rather than fix it printing again. The error handling improvement aligns with codebase conventions (BaseOpts, FluoExec, FluoCommand).

POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:90] The PR title "fixes #981 stopped printing meta info in scan" is misleading — the changes REMOVE meta info output ("Scanning snapshot of data...", "No data found") rather than restoring it. PR description should clarify the actual intent (e.g., removing noisy output vs fixing a bug).
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:127] Removing the "No data found" message means users get no feedback when a scan returns zero results — the CLI silently exits with code 0. This is poor UX; consider keeping a brief message like "No data found" or document this as intentional.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:132-133] Good improvement: changing `System.out.println("Scan failed - " + e.getMessage())` to `System.err.println(...) + System.exit(-1)` aligns with the majority pattern in CLI utilities (BaseOpts.java, FluoExec.java, FluoCommand.java all use stderr + exit(-1)).
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:171-172] Same error handling improvement for scanAccumulo is consistent and correct.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:127-128] The `System.exit(-1)` calls in both methods will terminate the JVM. Both callers (FluoScan.main() and deprecated AppRunner.scan()) are terminal paths so this is acceptable, but utility methods calling System.exit can be surprising — a comment explaining this design choice would help future maintainers.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:90] The `entriesFound` counter variable is cleanly removed with its associated "No data found" logic — no dangling references remain. Code cleanup is thorough.
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR removes user-facing status messages from scan utilities while improving error handling to use System.err and System.exit(-1), consistent with codebase conventions. However, the PR title claiming to fix "stopped printing meta info" directly contradicts the changes, which remove meta info printing entirely.

POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:90-93] **Title mismatch with changes**: PR title says "fixes #981 stopped printing meta info in scan" implying meta info stopped appearing and needs restoration, but the diff REMOVES the "Scanning snapshot of data..." and "Scanning data in Accumulo directly..." status messages. This is the opposite of what the title claims. The title or the changes need to be corrected.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:90-91,142-143] **Loss of user feedback for CLI tool**: These status messages inform users which scan mode is active (Fluo vs raw Accumulo) and which application is being scanned. Caller analysis of `FluoScan.java:105-107` and `AppRunner.java:76-78` shows neither caller prints progress info — these ScanUtil messages are the only user feedback. Removing them reduces CLI usability, especially since scans can take time before producing output.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:128-130] **Removal of "No data found" feedback**: The `entriesFound` counter and "No data found" message provided explicit confirmation when a scan returned no results. Without it, users may be uncertain whether the tool completed successfully or silently failed. Consider keeping this message.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:132-134,170-172] **Good: Error handling aligns with codebase convention**: Changing `System.out.println` to `System.err.println` and adding `System.exit(-1)` is consistent with the dominant pattern across CLI tools in this repository (confirmed in FluoExec.java:48-50, FluoInit.java:125-132, BaseOpts.java:32-35, FluoCommand.java:108-114). This change is correct and should be kept.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:90] **Trailing whitespace cleanup is benign**: The whitespace removal in the license header is a minor formatting fix with no functional impact.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR removes informational print statements and adds `System.exit(-1)` on scan failures. The PR title ("stopped printing meta info") contradicts the diff which removes meta info. Additionally, the new `System.exit(-1)` calls could terminate non-CLI callers unexpectedly.

POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:93] **PR title contradicts the diff**: The title says "fixes #981 stopped printing meta info in scan", but this diff REMOVES the meta info messages ("Scanning snapshot of data..." and "No data found"). If meta info stopped printing, the fix should restore those messages, not remove them. Please clarify the intent or fix the PR title.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:129-130] **`System.exit(-1)` may affect non-CLI callers**: The subagent found that `AppRunner.java` (modules/cluster/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/cluster/runner/AppRunner.java:75-78) calls `ScanUtil.scanFluo()` from an instance method, not a CLI main method. Adding `System.exit(-1)` on `FluoException` could unexpectedly terminate an application that embeds this utility. Consider throwing a runtime exception instead, or at minimum document that callers must handle this termination behavior. Note: the existing `System.exit(-1)` for `IllegalArgumentException` (line 107) has the same risk, but was already present before this PR.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:171-172] **Same `System.exit(-1)` concern for `scanAccumulo`**: `AppRunner.java` also calls `scanAccumulo()` (line 75-78). Same risk of unexpected JVM termination for non-CLI embedding scenarios.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:127-128] **Removal of "No data found" message may confuse users**: This informational message helped users distinguish between "scan returned no results" vs "scan produced no output for another reason". If intentional removal, consider whether users would benefit from retaining it.

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:129, 171] **Good: Error messages now go to `System.err`**: Changing from `System.out.println` to `System.err.println` for failure messages aligns with codebase conventions (matches `FluoWorkerImpl`, `FluoOracleImpl`, `FluoExec`, and `FluoWait`).

- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java] **No test coverage for `scanFluo`/`scanAccumulo`**: The existing test `ScanTest.java` only tests `getSpan()` and `getColumns()`. Consider adding integration tests for error paths if `System.exit(-1)` behavior is intentional.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR intentionally removes metadata print statements from scan operations (fixing #981) and improves error handling consistency by switching scan failures from `System.out` to `System.err` with `System.exit(-1)`. The changes are well-aligned with established CLI conventions in the codebase.

POINTS:
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:130] The change from `System.out.println` to `System.err.println` + `System.exit(-1)` for `FluoException` is consistent with the dominant error handling pattern in `FluoCommand.java:35-37,108-114`, `BaseOpts.java:33-35`, and `FluoExec.java:49-50`. Good improvement.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:171] Same improvement for `scanAccumulo` — now consistent with `scanFluo`. Note: both callers (`FluoScan.java:105,107` and `AppRunner.java:76,78`) invoke these methods without try-catch, so `System.exit(-1)` will terminate the JVM directly, which is appropriate for CLI tools.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:128] Consider also addressing `CommandUtil.java:28-30,38-40`, which still uses `System.out.println()` for error messages ("Application not initialized"/"Application not running"). This is the only file in the CLI layer that prints errors to stdout — could be flagged as a follow-up for consistency.
- [modules/command/src/test/java/org/apache/fluo/command/ScanTest.java] Pre-existing gap: only `getSpan()` and `getColumns()` are tested. The removed "No data found" message and the new `System.exit(-1)` behavior in `scanFluo`/`scanAccumulo` are untested. Not blocking, but worth noting for future coverage.
- [modules/core/src/main/java/org/apache/fluo/core/util/ScanUtil.java:119] The `IllegalArgumentException` handler already used `System.err.println()` + `System.exit(-1)` in the base code — this PR makes the other exception handlers match it. Clean consolidation.
